sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 8:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 8:1
items.html: Because although the lights departed from the Guf, they still receive enough light to sustain them. They receive this illumination through the Keter that departed from them and rose under Malchut of the Rosh, since it is the root of every ten Sefirot of the Guf, as the light of Keter always contains all the ten Sefirot that expand through it. (Item 3, and Inner Light there)
